bullets.text: U.S. banking agencies clarified capital treatment for tokenized securities, giving banks clearer rules for holding/issuing tokenized assets.
Fed ended its enforcement action with Wells Fargo, implying remediation progress and potentially lowering regulatory overhang for the bank.
U.S. productivity rose 2.8% in Q4 2025 while unit labor costs also rose 2.8%, suggesting productivity gains didn’t ease wage-driven inflation pressure.
Iran war pushed U.S. crude above $80/bbl as supply disruptions intensified, raising global inflation risks and pressuring fuel-sensitive sectors.
House rejected a war-powers resolution on Iran, reinforcing presidential flexibility and increasing tail risk of escalation and prolonged conflict.
Iran delayed naming a new leader for security reasons, signaling internal instability and heightened information control during wartime conditions.
U.S. tariff policy faces legal whiplash: a judge ordered refunding $130B+ in tariffs while states sued to block new tariffs, boosting trade uncertainty.
Pentagon labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk; Anthropic plans to challenge in court, highlighting rising national-security scrutiny of frontier AI vendors.
U.S. reportedly weighing sweeping new AI chip export controls; Nvidia slid, underscoring tighter U.S. gatekeeping and further semiconductor market fragmentation.
OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 (including Pro and Thinking versions), intensifying competition and likely increasing demand for compute, data-center capacity, and AI tooling.
AI chip demand narrative strengthened as Marvell and Broadcom beats/rallies reinforced expectations that AI infrastructure spending remains durable.
Amazon said a software deployment triggered an hours-long outage, highlighting operational concentration risk in large digital commerce and cloud-linked systems.
Home sellers are relisting at the fastest pace in a decade, but spring inventory remains low—supporting prices even as affordability remains strained.
New rare-epilepsy drug cut seizures by up to 91% in children, signaling a potentially significant therapeutic advance with major quality-of-life and cost impacts.
Study warns about half of Amazon insects may face dangerous heat stress, underscoring escalating biodiversity risks from warming and potential impacts on ecosystems.
